title: betula pubescens f. urticifolia
intro: Betula pubescens f. urticifolia, also known as the nettle-leaved birch or urticated birch, is a variation of the Betula pubescens tree, characterized by its leaves resembling those of the nettle plant. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis, kidney stones, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Betula pubescens f. urticifolia include reducing swelling and pain, improving kidney function, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Betula pubescens f. urticifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
